signals.headline: Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2
signals.raw:
Germany March 2026 Inflation Confirmed at 2.7% YoY | YES | HIGH confidence**  
Germany's annual inflation rate rose to 2.7% in March 2026 from 1.9% in February, confirming preliminary estimates and marking the highest since January 2024, driven by a 7.2% energy price surge (fuel +20%, heating oil +44.4%).[1] This exceeds the ECB's ~2% euro area HICP projection for late 2025 and could shift markets
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation at 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation at 2.2% YoY, Below Forecasts | YES | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% prior, missing market expectations of 2.4% while aligning with the Bank of Korea's 2.2% yearly outlook amid Middle East energy shocks.[2] This softer-than-expected print may ease rate hike bets despite import vulnerabilities.[2]

signals.headline: US, Iran Plan Fresh Ceasefire Talks Before April 7 Agreement Expires Next Week | NO | MED confidence**
